LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JONATHAN HERNANDEZ DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT SAM INCOPORATED'S ANSWER. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. IF INSUFFICIENT FACTS CURRENTLY EXIST, DEFENDANT MAY BRING A MOTION TO AMEND THE ANSWER AT A LATER DATE.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER). JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JONATHAN HERNANDEZ'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Beth A. Huber of the Huber Law Firm (456.4411) for defendant and opposing party; Stephen A. Sommers of Kumin Sommers LLP (434.4500) for plaintiff and moving party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion for leave to amend denied. The application is untimely per Labor Code 2699.3(a)(2)(c). Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
